patches.find: PROSPECTIVE — Predicted Before Data" (9 WINs) from backtested predictions, and the predictions page defines prospective as carrying "highest evidential weight." By the dome's own accounting, 87% of its claimed successes are admittedly retrodictions.
patches.replace: PROSPECTIVE — Predicted Before Data" ({{DOME_PROSPECTIVE_COUNT}} WINs) from backtested predictions, and the predictions page defines prospective as carrying "highest evidential weight." By the dome's own accounting, only {{DOME_PROSPECTIVE_COUNT}} of its {{TOTAL_WINS}} claimed successes are prospective — making the overwhelming majority (over 85%) admittedly retrodictions.
patches.rationale: Fixes two issues in one patch: (1) hardcoded '(9 WINs)' now uses {{DOME_PROSPECTIVE_COUNT}} template variable (ISS-1018); (2) hardcoded '87%' is stale — dome_claimed_prospective_count is 10, making retrodictions ~85.7%, so replaced with template variables and 'over 85%' conservative bound (ISS-1019)

patches.find: WIN-044 is self-contradicted by the model's own refraction fix
patches.replace: WIN-044 is self-contradicted by the coordinate collapse triggered by the dome's refraction fix (Section 1.8)
patches.rationale: Harmonizes the two descriptions of WIN-044's failure mode. First mention says 'coordinate collapse' (FSF cannot be evaluated without resolving ray-tracing geometry). Second mention said 'model's own refraction fix'. Now both use the same framing: 'coordinate collapse triggered by the dome's refraction fix (Section 1.8)'

patches.find: Of all reviewed WINs, only a handful derive their predicted values from the dome's own geometric equations — and even those use parameters fitted to reproduce already-known values. The rest are retrodictions: the author looked up the answer, then wrote a "prediction" matching it. Without a derivation chain from dome geometry to predicted value, a correct number is curve-fitting, not prediction.
patches.replace: Of all reviewed WINs, only a handful derive their predicted values from the dome's own geometric equations — and even those fail on examination: one is self-contradicted by the dome's own coordinate collapse, the other is a coordinate-system definition rather than a physical prediction. The rest are retrodictions: the author looked up the answer, then wrote a "prediction" matching it. Without a derivation chain from dome geometry to predicted value, a correct number is curve-fitting, not prediction.
patches.rationale: Fixes TLDR imprecision: the body shows WIN-044 is a genuine V12-geometry derivation that is self-contradicted (not merely 'curve-fitting'), and WIN-057 is definitional. The new TLDR accurately describes why the handful of dome-derived WINs fail, matching the body's actual analysis.

patches.find: The remaining {{CA_REVIEWED}} minus {{CA_DOME}} predictions state the already-known answer without connecting it to the model's equations.
patches.replace: The remaining predictions (all but the {{CA_DOME}} dome-derived) state the already-known answer without connecting it to the model's equations.
patches.rationale: Removes exposed arithmetic expression 'remaining {{CA_REVIEWED}} minus {{CA_DOME}}' which renders as '70 minus 2' — a visible subtraction expression. Rephrased as 'all but the {{CA_DOME}} dome-derived' which renders naturally as 'all but the 2 dome-derived'.
